description: Paired 3-seed coupon intensity and pricing-form audit at the 12-vehicle nine-zone baseline.

distance_fare_analysis.classification: short <= 5 km; medium > 5 and <= 10 km; long > 10 km
distance_fare_analysis.scope_note: Bands are transparent experiment reporting bins, not calibrated policy thresholds.

intensity.description: Hold C3 recipients fixed and compare no coupon, 10, 20 and 30 percent discounts.

format.description: Hold C3 recipients fixed and compare 20 percent off, 5 yuan off and 30 yuan threshold with 5 yuan off.

equal_budget.description: Hold C3 recipients and a 50 yuan maximum daily subsidy liability fixed across pricing forms.

high_value_format.description: Compare uncapped 20 percent discount with a stronger flat 10 yuan coupon and a 50-minus-10 high-fare threshold coupon.

scope_notes: All non-baseline scenarios within a seed use exactly the same C3 coupon recipients.
K30_5 eligibility is evaluated on original pre-coupon fare; an ineligible request does not bind, redeem or consume the daily coupon.
Coupon count is held fixed, but total subsidy is not budget-equal across pricing forms and must be reported separately.
Pricing-form results must be reported by request distance, original fare, absolute discount and intrazonal/interzonal scope; they must not be attributed to the threshold alone.
The equal-budget set fixes maximum fiscal liability rather than forcing equal realized spending: 10 awarded coupons times a 5 yuan cap equals at most 50 yuan per day.

The high-value-format set is an additional sensitivity analysis: 50-minus-10 equals a 20 percent discount only at a 50 yuan original fare, while remaining unavailable below 50 and capped at 10 above 50.
This is a mechanism audit, not a calibrated Shanghai coupon programme.
